summary: ts-webplug 3.0.6 fetches and executes remote JavaScript from jsonkeeper.com via detached child process
details: The package masquerades as a pino-compatible logger (module.exports.pino = middleware, mirrored lib/ file layout including proto.js, levels.js, redaction.js, transport.js, worker.js) but its exported middleware factory spawns a detached `node./lib/caller.js` on first invocation. caller.js fetches a JavaScript payload from https://jsonkeeper.com/b/D3GLF (with additional base64-encoded fallback URLs https://jsonkeeper.com/b/XRGF3 and https://jsonkeeper.com/b/4NAKK) and executes the response's `.cookie` field via `new Function.constructor('require', s)(require)`, giving the remote author arbitrary code execution with full `require` access inside the consumer's Node process. The C2 URLs are concealed behind a fake `process.env`-shaped object with base64-encoded values (DEV_API_KEY etc.) in lib/caller.js and lib/const.js, alongside decoy `x-secret-key` header material — a deliberate cover-story to hide the endpoint. jsonkeeper.com is a mutable anonymous paste host, so the executed payload can change at any time without a package update.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
